decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to assemble the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This mana is used to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Devourer of Destiny, as well as control cards like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Trinisphere and Kozilek's Command, while also utilizing Ugin's Labyrinth to maintain board control. Ultimately, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with large threats and powerful removal spells.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls. The deck also includes key support cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to deal damage and control the board. Ranger-Captain of Eos helps tutor for important creatures, while Haliya, Guided by Light offers protection and evasion. The deck has a strong mana base with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistency in casting spells. Overall, this deck aims to be fast and efficient in dealing damage to the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly dump cards into the graveyard to enable the casting of powerful creatures like Hollow One, Ox of Agonas, and Detective's Phoenix at reduced costs. By using cards like Faithless Looting, Burning Inquiry, and Marauding Mako, the deck aims to create explosive turns and overwhelm opponents with early aggression. Lightning Bolt and Dragon's Rage Channeler provide additional removal and card advantage, while the inclusion of Cool but Rude and Fear of Missing Out helps to maintain pressure on the opponent. Ultimately, the goal is to close out the game swiftly and decisively.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively disrupt the opponent's hand and board state using cards like Inquisition of Kozilek, Thoughtseize, and Fatal Push, while maintaining resource advantage with Mishra's Bauble and Kozilek's Command. The deck also aims to apply pressure with efficient beaters like Thought-Knot Seer, Nethergoyf, and Emperor of Bones. Additionally, it utilizes land synergies with Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Eldrazi Temple to cheat out threats and generate value. The inclusion of cards like Shadowspear and Ifnir Deadlands provide additional utility and flexibility in different matchups.

This deck focuses on assembling the Amulet Titan combo by utilizing bounce lands and Amulet of Vigor to generate extra mana and cheat out Primeval Titan. The goal is to ramp quickly and efficiently to get Primeval Titan onto the battlefield, fetching lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Gruul Turf to support the combo. With the right setup, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with powerful land drops and combo off for a victory. The deck also includes some toolbox cards like Green Sun's Zenith to tutor for specific answers or threats as needed.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combination of Basking Broodscale, Eldrazi Temple, Glaring Fleshraker, and Devourer of Destiny to generate powerful mana ramp and combo potential. By using cards like Kozilek's Command, Malevolent Rumble, and Emrakul, the Promised End, it aims to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of cards like Ugin's Labyrinth and Boseiju, Who Endures provides additional protection and utility. With the right setup and timing, this deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with its synergistic combos and powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on assembling a strong board presence quickly by playing cheap artifacts and utilizing synergies with cards like Urza's Saga and Emry, Lurker of the Loch. By generating tokens with Pinnacle Emissary and using Kappa Cannoneer for additional damage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. The inclusion of utility cards like Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt helps in dealing with various threats, while the presence of cards like Shadowspear and Loki, God of Mischief provides additional ways to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

Utilizing a mixture of counterspells, removal, and card advantage, this UW Control deck aims to control the game by limiting the opponent's options and resources. By protecting key threats like Narset, Parter of Veils and Teferi, Time Raveler while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Solitude and Orim's Chant, the deck can outvalue and outpace the opponent. The inclusion of Isochron Scepter and Consult the Star Charts allows for additional card advantage and flexibility in dealing with threats. The deck's goal is to stall the game until it can establish a dominant position and close out the game with powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize the synergy between creature cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Basking Broodscale with equipment like Blade of the Bloodchief to create a powerful combo. The deck also includes cards like Emrakul, the Promised End and Kozilek's Command for additional control and disruption. The inclusion of cards like Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Saga helps to generate card advantage and find key pieces of the combo quickly. Overall, the deck focuses on assembling the combo pieces efficiently while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The key idea for this deck is to use a variety of creature toolbox cards in order to assemble a powerful combo. By utilizing cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Chord of Calling, the player can search for specific creatures needed at any given moment. Yawgmoth, Thran Physician serves as a powerful engine for the deck, allowing for card draw and sacrificing creatures for various effects. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Malevolent Rumble and Spymaster's Vault to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to synergize its creatures and combo off for a big win.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly generate mana and overwhelm the opponent with early aggression. It also includes disruptive elements such as Pithing Needle and Tormod's Crypt to interact with the opponent's strategy. Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides recursion and card advantage, while Urza's Saga helps to tutor for key artifacts. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence with efficient creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary, while also having access to removal and protection through cards like Engineered Explosives and Welding Jar.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive plays, utilizing cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Slickshot Show-Off to quickly overwhelm opponents. Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration help maintain card advantage, while Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart provide efficient removal and direct damage. The deck aims to control the board early on and maintain pressure throughout the game, with Monastery Swiftspear and Mutagenic Growth serving as additional threats. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to use its speed and efficiency to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in a fast-paced Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly and efficiently to cast a series of powerful spells, including Desperate Ritual, Pyretic Ritual, and Manamorphose. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces, such as Ruby Medallion and Wrenn's Resolve, to generate an explosive amount of mana and "storm off" with cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Reckless Impulse. The deck also includes cards like Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard and maintain momentum. The key strategy is to chain together spells and generate a game-winning amount of damage or value in a single turn.

The deck focuses on using Allosaurus Rider to generate mana and search for combo pieces like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant and Griselbrand. It utilizes cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Eldritch Evolution to ramp and find key pieces quickly. The deck also includes protection spells like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to ensure the combo goes off smoothly. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and game-winning abilities.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by removal spells like Thraben Charm and Galvanic Discharge. The deck also includes disruption elements like Blood Moon and Ranger-Captain of Eos to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, with a mix of fetchlands and shocklands. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain board control to secure a quick victory.

This Boros Ponza deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while controlling the board through land destruction and removal spells. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's resources and win conditions, ultimately taking over the game with powerful threats like Solitude and Wrath of the Skies. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion provides additional synergy and a powerful creature option. Overall, the deck seeks to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent while maintaining control of the battlefield.
